content.content:
1	\paragraph{1993 A--1 \textnormal{[\texttt{integral-evaluation}]}}
2	The horizontal line $y=c$ intersects the curve $y = 2x - 3x^3$
3	in the first quadrant as in the figure. Find $c$ so that the areas of the
4	two shaded regions are equal. [Figure not included. The first region is
5	bounded by the $y$-axis, the line $y=c$ and the curve; the other lies
6	under the curve and above the line $y=c$ between their two points of
7	intersection.]
8

file.content:
\paragraph{1993 A--1 \textnormal{[\texttt{integral-evaluation}]}}
The horizontal line $y=c$ intersects the curve $y = 2x - 3x^3$
in the first quadrant as in the figure. Find $c$ so that the areas of the
two shaded regions are equal. [Figure not included. The first region is
bounded by the $y$-axis, the line $y=c$ and the curve; the other lies
under the curve and above the line $y=c$ between their two points of
intersection.]

input.content:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory intervalIntegral

noncomputable def putnam_1993_a1_solution : ℝ := 4 / 9

theorem putnam_1993_a1 :
    0 < putnam_1993_a1_solution ∧
    putnam_1993_a1_solution < 4 * Real.sqrt 2 / 9 ∧
    (∃ x1 x2 : ℝ, x1 < x2 ∧
      2 * x1 - 3 * x1 ^ 3 = putnam_1993_a1_solution ∧
      2 * x2 - 3 * x2 ^ 3 = putnam_1993_a1_solution ∧
      0 ≤ x1 ∧
      (∫ x in x1..x2, (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3 - putnam_1993_a1_solution)) =
        (∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..x1, (putnam_1993_a1_solution - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3)))) := by
  have hs3 : Real.sqrt 3 ^ 2 = 3 := Real.sq_sqrt (by norm_num)
  have hs3nn : (0 : ℝ) ≤ Real.sqrt 3 := Real.sqrt_nonneg 3
  have hs2 : Real.sqrt 2 ^ 2 = 2 := Real.sq_sqrt (by norm_num)
  have hs2nn : (0 : ℝ) ≤ Real.sqrt 2 := Real.sqrt_nonneg 2
  -- antiderivative of  2x - 3x^3 - 4/9
  have hF : ∀ x : ℝ,
      HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y)
        (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3 - 4 / 9) x := by
    intro x
    have h1 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2) ((2 : ℕ) * x ^ (2 - 1)) x :=
      hasDerivAt_pow 2 x
    have h2 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => 3 / 4 * y ^ 4)
        ((3 / 4 : ℝ) * ((4 : ℕ) * x ^ (4 - 1))) x :=
      (hasDerivAt_pow 4 x).const_mul (3 / 4 : ℝ)
    have h3 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => 4 / 9 * y) ((4 / 9 : ℝ) * 1) x :=
      (hasDerivAt_id x).const_mul (4 / 9 : ℝ)
    convert (h1.sub h2).sub h3 using 1
    push_cast
    ring
  refine ⟨by norm_num [putnam_1993_a1_solution], ?_, (Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3, 2 / 3, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- 4/9 < 4 √2 / 9
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution]
    nlinarith [hs2, hs2nn]
  · -- x1 < x2
    nlinarith [hs3, hs3nn]
  · -- 2 x1 - 3 x1^3 = 4/9
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution]
    nlinarith [hs3, hs3nn]
  · -- 2 x2 - 3 x2^3 = 4/9
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution]; norm_num
  · -- 0 ≤ x1
    nlinarith [hs3, hs3nn]
  · -- the integral identity
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution]
    have hL : (∫ x in (Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3..(2 / 3 : ℝ),
        (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3 - 4 / 9)) =
        (fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y) (2 / 3)
          - (fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y) ((Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3) := by
      apply intervalIntegral.integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt
      · intro x _; exact hF x
      · apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable; fun_prop
    have hR : (∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..(Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3,
        (4 / 9 - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3))) =
        (fun y : ℝ => -(y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y)) ((Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3)
          - (fun y : ℝ => -(y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y)) 0 := by
      apply intervalIntegral.integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt
      · intro x _
        have := (hF x).neg
        convert this using 1
        ring
      · apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable; fun_prop
    rw [hL, hR]
    simp only
    nlinarith [hs3, hs3nn]

input.content:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory intervalIntegral

noncomputable def putnam_1993_a1_solution : ℝ := 4 / 9

theorem putnam_1993_a1 :
    0 < putnam_1993_a1_solution ∧
    putnam_1993_a1_solution < 4 * Real.sqrt 2 / 9 ∧
    (∃ x1 x2 : ℝ, x1 < x2 ∧
      2 * x1 - 3 * x1 ^ 3 = putnam_1993_a1_solution ∧
      2 * x2 - 3 * x2 ^ 3 = putnam_1993_a1_solution ∧
      0 ≤ x1 ∧
      (∫ x in x1..x2, (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3 - putnam_1993_a1_solution)) =
        (∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..x1, (putnam_1993_a1_solution - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3)))) := by
  have hs3 : Real.sqrt 3 ^ 2 = 3 := Real.sq_sqrt (by norm_num)
  have hs3nn : (0 : ℝ) ≤ Real.sqrt 3 := Real.sqrt_nonneg 3
  have hs2 : Real.sqrt 2 ^ 2 = 2 := Real.sq_sqrt (by norm_num)
  have hs2nn : (0 : ℝ) ≤ Real.sqrt 2 := Real.sqrt_nonneg 2
  -- antiderivative of  2x - 3x^3 - 4/9
  have hF : ∀ x : ℝ,
      HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y)
        (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3 - 4 / 9) x := by
    intro x
    have h1 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2) ((2 : ℕ) * x ^ (2 - 1)) x :=
      hasDerivAt_pow 2 x
    have h2 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => 3 / 4 * y ^ 4)
        ((3 / 4 : ℝ) * ((4 : ℕ) * x ^ (4 - 1))) x :=
      (hasDerivAt_pow 4 x).const_mul (3 / 4 : ℝ)
    have h3 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => 4 / 9 * y) ((4 / 9 : ℝ) * 1) x :=
      (hasDerivAt_id x).const_mul (4 / 9 : ℝ)
    convert (h1.sub h2).sub h3 using 1
    push_cast
    ring
  refine ⟨by norm_num [putnam_1993_a1_solution], ?_, (Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3, 2 / 3, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- 4/9 < 4 √2 / 9
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution]
    nlinarith [hs2, hs2nn]
  · -- x1 < x2
    nlinarith [hs3, hs3nn]
  · -- 2 x1 - 3 x1^3 = 4/9
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution]
    nlinarith [hs3, hs3nn]
  · -- 2 x2 - 3 x2^3 = 4/9
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution]; norm_num
  · -- 0 ≤ x1
    nlinarith [hs3, hs3nn]
  · -- the integral identity
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution]
    have hL : (∫ x in (Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3..(2 / 3 : ℝ),
        (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3 - 4 / 9)) =
        (fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y) (2 / 3)
          - (fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y) ((Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3) := by
      apply intervalIntegral.integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt
      · intro x _; exact hF x
      · apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable; fun_prop
    have hR : (∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..(Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3,
        (4 / 9 - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3))) =
        (fun y : ℝ => -(y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y)) ((Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3)
          - (fun y : ℝ => -(y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y)) 0 := by
      apply intervalIntegral.integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt
      · intro x _
        have := (hF x).neg
        convert this using 1
        ring
      · apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable; fun_prop
    rw [hL, hR]
    simp only
    nlinarith [hs3, hs3nn]

content.text: The `apply` fails on higher-order unification. Let me restructure with explicit named arguments and helper lemmas.

input.content:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory intervalIntegral

noncomputable def putnam_1993_a1_solution : ℝ := 4 / 9

theorem putnam_1993_a1 :
    0 < putnam_1993_a1_solution ∧
    putnam_1993_a1_solution < 4 * Real.sqrt 2 / 9 ∧
    (∃ x1 x2 : ℝ, x1 < x2 ∧
      2 * x1 - 3 * x1 ^ 3 = putnam_1993_a1_solution ∧
      2 * x2 - 3 * x2 ^ 3 = putnam_1993_a1_solution ∧
      0 ≤ x1 ∧
      (∫ x in x1..x2, (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3 - putnam_1993_a1_solution)) =
        (∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..x1, (putnam_1993_a1_solution - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3)))) := by
  have hs3 : Real.sqrt 3 ^ 2 = 3 := Real.sq_sqrt (by norm_num)
  have hs3nn : (0 : ℝ) ≤ Real.sqrt 3 := Real.sqrt_nonneg 3
  have hs2 : Real.sqrt 2 ^ 2 = 2 := Real.sq_sqrt (by norm_num)
  have hs2nn : (0 : ℝ) ≤ Real.sqrt 2 := Real.sqrt_nonneg 2
  -- antiderivative of  2x - 3x^3 - 4/9
  have hF : ∀ x : ℝ,
      HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y)
        (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3 - 4 / 9) x := by
    intro x
    have h1 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2) ((2 : ℕ) * x ^ (2 - 1)) x :=
      hasDerivAt_pow 2 x
    have h2 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => 3 / 4 * y ^ 4)
        ((3 / 4 : ℝ) * ((4 : ℕ) * x ^ (4 - 1))) x :=
      (hasDerivAt_pow 4 x).const_mul (3 / 4 : ℝ)
    have h3 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => 4 / 9 * y) ((4 / 9 : ℝ) * 1) x :=
      (hasDerivAt_id x).const_mul (4 / 9 : ℝ)
    convert (h1.sub h2).sub h3 using 1
    push_cast
    ring
  -- ∫ (2x - 3x^3 - 4/9) = P b - P a
  have key : ∀ a b : ℝ, (∫ x in a..b, (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3 - 4 / 9)) =
      (b ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * b ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * b) - (a ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * a ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * a) := by
    intro a b
    have h := intervalIntegral.integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt
      (f := fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y)
      (f' := fun x : ℝ => 2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3 - 4 / 9)
      (a := a) (b := b) (fun x _ => hF x)
      (by apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable; fun_prop)
    simpa using h
  -- ∫ (4/9 - (2x - 3x^3)) = -P b + P a
  have key2 : ∀ a b : ℝ, (∫ x in a..b, (4 / 9 - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3))) =
      (-(b ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * b ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * b)) - (-(a ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * a ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * a)) := by
    intro a b
    have hd : ∀ x : ℝ, HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => -(y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y))
        (4 / 9 - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3)) x := by
      intro x
      have := (hF x).neg
      convert this using 1
      ring
    have h := intervalIntegral.integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt
      (f := fun y : ℝ => -(y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y))
      (f' := fun x : ℝ => 4 / 9 - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3))
      (a := a) (b := b) (fun x _ => hd x)
      (by apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable; fun_prop)
    simpa using h
  refine ⟨by norm_num [putnam_1993_a1_solution], ?_, (Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3, 2 / 3, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- 4/9 < 4 √2 / 9
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution]
    nlinarith [hs2, hs2nn]
  · -- x1 < x2
    nlinarith [hs3, hs3nn]
  · -- 2 x1 - 3 x1^3 = 4/9
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution]
    linear_combination ((3 - Real.sqrt 3) / 9) * hs3
  · -- 2 x2 - 3 x2^3 = 4/9
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution]; norm_num
  · -- 0 ≤ x1
    nlinarith [hs3, hs3nn]
  · -- the integral identity
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution, key, key2]
    ring

  have hs3nn : (0 : ℝ) ≤ Real.sqrt 3 := Real.sqrt_nonneg 3
  have hs2 : Real.sqrt 2 ^ 2 = 2 := Real.sq_sqrt (by norm_num)
  have hs2nn : (0 : ℝ) ≤ Real.sqrt 2 := Real.sqrt_nonneg 2

    have h2 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => 3 / 4 * y ^ 4)

import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory intervalIntegral

noncomputable def putnam_1993_a1_solution : ℝ := 4 / 9

theorem putnam_1993_a1 :
    0 < putnam_1993_a1_solution ∧
    putnam_1993_a1_solution < 4 * Real.sqrt 2 / 9 ∧
    (∃ x1 x2 : ℝ, x1 < x2 ∧
      2 * x1 - 3 * x1 ^ 3 = putnam_1993_a1_solution ∧
      2 * x2 - 3 * x2 ^ 3 = putnam_1993_a1_solution ∧
      0 ≤ x1 ∧
      (∫ x in x1..x2, (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3 - putnam_1993_a1_solution)) =
        (∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..x1, (putnam_1993_a1_solution - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3)))) := by
  have hs3 : Real.sqrt 3 ^ 2 = 3 := Real.sq_sqrt (by norm_num)
  have hs3nn : (0 : ℝ) ≤ Real.sqrt 3 := Real.sqrt_nonneg 3
  have hs2 : Real.sqrt 2 ^ 2 = 2 := Real.sq_sqrt (by norm_num)
  have hs2nn : (0 : ℝ) ≤ Real.sqrt 2 := Real.sqrt_nonneg 2
  -- antiderivative of  2x - 3x^3 - 4/9
  have hF : ∀ x : ℝ,
      HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y)
        (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3 - 4 / 9) x := by
    intro x
    have h1 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2) ((2 : ℕ) * x ^ (2 - 1)) x :=
      hasDerivAt_pow 2 x
    have h2 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => 3 / 4 * y ^ 4)
        ((3 / 4 : ℝ) * ((4 : ℕ) * x ^ (4 - 1))) x :=
      (hasDerivAt_pow 4 x).const_mul (3 / 4 : ℝ)
    have h3 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => 4 / 9 * y) ((4 / 9 : ℝ) * 1) x :=
      (hasDerivAt_id x).const_mul (4 / 9 : ℝ)
    convert (h1.sub h2).sub h3 using 1
    push_cast
    ring
  refine ⟨by norm_num [putnam_1993_a1_solution], ?_, (Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3, 2 / 3, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- 4/9 < 4 √2 / 9
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution]
    nlinarith [hs2, hs2nn]
  · -- x1 < x2
    nlinarith [hs3, hs3nn]
  · -- 2 x1 - 3 x1^3 = 4/9
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution]
    nlinarith [hs3, hs3nn]
  · -- 2 x2 - 3 x2^3 = 4/9
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution]; norm_num
  · -- 0 ≤ x1
    nlinarith [hs3, hs3nn]
  · -- the integral identity
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution]
    have hL : (∫ x in (Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3..(2 / 3 : ℝ),
        (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3 - 4 / 9)) =
        (fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y) (2 / 3)
          - (fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y) ((Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3) := by
      apply intervalIntegral.integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt
      · intro x _; exact hF x
      · apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable; fun_prop
    have hR : (∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..(Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3,
        (4 / 9 - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3))) =
        (fun y : ℝ => -(y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y)) ((Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3)
          - (fun y : ℝ => -(y ^ 2 - 3 / 4 * y ^ 4 - 4 / 9 * y)) 0 := by
      apply intervalIntegral.integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt
      · intro x _
        have := (hF x).neg
        convert this using 1
        ring
      · apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable; fun_prop
    rw [hL, hR]
    simp only
    nlinarith [hs3, hs3nn]

    nlinarith [hs3, hs3nn]
  · -- the integral identity
    rw [putnam_1993_a1_solution, key, key2]
    ring
